mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of observations during early-cycle recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing one million units annually indicates strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which lifts equity markets over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain negative in more than 50 percent of quarters when housing starts cross the one million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding two hundred thousand workers confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, prompting immediate positive equity repricing over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200.0 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances when monthly payroll gains exceed two hundred thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with a static federal funds rate erodes real yields and forces anticipated monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of months when inflation accelerates while policy rates hold steady.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a rolling 3-year window.
